titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Pablo Echaurren Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 27, 2024 at 23:22

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of artwork, notebooks, writings, correspondence, clippings, magazines, photographs, posters, books, and audiovisual and computer media by or related to Italian artist Pablo Echaurren.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research
Box 13 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 14 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from L'Arengario Studio Bibliographico on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2013, 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Papers Related to Movimento del '77, 1973-2002. II. Printed Material, 1974-2013. III. Metroposter, 1993-2015. IV. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1997-2007.

bioghist.head: Pablo Echaurren (1951-)
bioghist.p: Pablo Echaurren is an Italian artist, author, activist, and art critic. In the late 1970s, he was involved with the Movimento del '77 and the Indiani Metropolitani and published zines such as Oask?! , Abat/jour , and Wam . He also contributed drawings to the radical leftist newspaper Lotta continua . In the 1980s and 1990s, Echaurren began drawing comic strips for magazines such as Frigidaire , Linus , Tango , Comic Art , and Alter Alter . Echaurren and his wife, art historian Claudia Salaris, have collaborated on several books on the Movimento del '77. His other works include the graphic novels Caffeina d'Europa and Majakovskij , as well as the illustration for the cover of Porci con le Ali . More recently Echaurren has returned to painting and also writes about art for the Huffington Post .

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Pablo Echaurren Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of artwork, notebooks, writings, correspondence, clippings, magazines, photographs, posters, books, and audiovisual and computer media by or related to Italian artist Pablo Echaurren. The bulk of materials document Echaurren's participation in the Movimento del '77 and include over 200 original black and white or color drawings by Echaurren for the radical leftist paper Lotta continua ; copies of zines he published and original offset plates for OASK?! ; and photographs of Echaurren and Claudia Salaris at the National Conference against Repression by Tano D'Amico. The collection also contains papers concerning Echaurren's involvement with the Indiani Metropolitani and the group's occupation of an apartment building in Rome, known as Casa del Desiderio, from May to December 1977. Included are writings, notes, and artwork documenting daily life and activities at Casa del Desiderio, which also functioned as an editorial office for OASK?! Later materials include letters and writings by individuals who were involved in the Movimento del '77 or contributed to OASK?! , such as Antonio Negri, Maurizio Gabbiannelli, Fiamma Lolli, Massimo Pasquini, Adriano Sofri, and Olivier Torquet. The collection additionally contains artwork and printed material related to "Metroposter," a joint project of Echaurren and Renato Curcio to create posters for the Rome metro. Included are original drawings by Echaurren and signed posters, and copies of the Italian magazine Frigidaire , which published Metroposters as inserts in multiple issues from 1993 to 1995.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Pablo Echaurren Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains posters, artwork, notes, printed material, poems, and other papers removed from the Casa del Desiderio when its occupants were evicted by the police in December 1977, as well as notebooks, one sketchbook, and over 200 original drawings by Pablo Echaurren for Lotta continua . Also included are letters to Echaurren from Maurizio Gabbianelli, Fiamma Lolli, Antonio (Toni) Negri, Massimo Pasquini, Adriano Sofri, and Olivier Torquet, and photographs by Tano D'Amico of Echaurren, Claudia Salaris, and Gabbianelli in Bologna in 1977. The series additionally contains a number of zines and fliers associated with Movimento del '77, most of which are annotated by Echaurren, including Altrove materiali , Enig/mistica , and OASK?!

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Lotta continua - sketch related to "Orsottantotto," published in December 24, 1977 issue
Empty envelope with Echaurren's description of select collection materials
Re nudo Pop Festival No. 3 poster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes books and exhibition catalogs by or related to Pablo Echaurren. Included are his works on Movimento del '77 and other topics, as well as books featuring illustration by Echaurren, such as Porci con le Ali and La violenza illustrata .

did.unittitle: Avanguardia di massa by Maurizio Calvesi
C'era, cioè c'è by Claudia Salaris and Pablo Echaurren

title.part: Care compagne, cari compagni: le donne, i cavalier, l'arme, gli amori, le cortesie, l'audaci imprese io canto ... : la storia del 77 in 350 lettere : lettere a Lotta continua

did.unittitle: Casa del Desiderio: '77: Indiani Metropolitani e altri strani by Pablo Echaurren

title.part: DeriveApprodi #15: Lingue & linguaggi gli Indiani Metropolitani

did.unittitle: Di versi by Gandalf il Viola [alias of Olivier Torquet]
Felce e mirtillo: dalla Beat generation agli Indiani Metropolitani (1967-1977) by Pablo Echaurren and Guido Pautasso
Matta: Roberto Sebastian Matta, Gordon Matta-Clark, Pablo Echaurren edited by Danilo Eccher

title.part: Oltreconfine: Indiani Metropolitani, maodadaisti e altri avventuristi a Roma

did.unittitle: Pablo Echaurren a Siena by Claudia Casali
Pablo Echaurren crhomo sapiens by Nicoletta Zanella
Pablo Echaurren: dagli anni settanta a oggi edited by F. Benzi, F. Pirani, C. Salaris
Pablo Echaurren perizia calligrafica [artist's book with text by Adriano Spatola, dedicated to Echaurren]
Parole ribelli: i fogli del Movimento del 77 edited by Pablo Echaurren
Porci con le Ali: diario sessuo-politico di due adolescenti by Marco Lombardo-Radice
Riviste futuriste: collezione Echaurren Salaris by Claudia Salaris
La violenza illustrata by Nanni Balestrini

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials relating to "Metroposter," a joint project of Pablo Echaurren and Renato Curcio in 1993-1994. Included are issues of Frigidaire magazine with Metroposter inserts and original artwork for the posters, as well as a book about the project and a collection of essays by Curcio.

did.unittitle: Frigidaire Metroposter inserts, issue no. 1-13 [no. 14 not included]
Metroposters signed by Echaurren and Curcio

title.part: Metroposter : Parole di Renato Curcio e disegni di Pablo Echaurren per i viaggiatori della metropolitana di Roma

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of a DVD with an interview of Pablo Echaurren by journalist Claudio Sabelli Fioretti, and a vinyl record containing music for "Porci con le ali," featuring original art by Echaurren on the album sleeve.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Porci con le Ali colonna sonora originale del film [album sleeve signed by Echaurren]

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
